title: rubus diminutus
intro: Rubus diminutus, also known as the dwarf raspberry or dwarf thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the western United States. The medicinal properties of Rubus diminutus include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of consuming Rubus diminutus berries include their high content of vitamins C and K, as well as their potential to support cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Rubus diminutus can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion, tincture, or decoction, and is often used to make teas, jams, and other medicinal products.
